summary: PR 2444 primarily decompiled and improved menu main-rule code in src/melee/mn/mnmainrule.c, updated mnmainrule.h with recovered struct fields and prototypes, and made small formatting-only-looking edits in grhomerun.c. The automated report credited 2 new matches in main/melee/mn/mnmainrule, 10 unmatched-item improvements, and one .sdata2 regression.

key_files.role: Main decompilation target; replaces several placeholder function comments with C bodies, adds local structs/data declarations, includes, animation tables, and menu-rule control/animation logic.
evidence_refs: changed_files: src/melee/mn/mnmainrule.c added 1015 deleted 63
diff: /// #fn_8022F538 through /// #mn_80230274 replaced with function bodies and data declarations
bot_report: new matches and improvements all in main/melee/mn/mnmainrule

key_files.role: Header/prototype update; expands struct mn_802307F8_t fields and replaces several UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS declarations with typed prototypes.
evidence_refs: changed_files: src/melee/mn/mnmainrule.h added 24 deleted 10
diff: struct mn_802307F8_t gains x3, x4, x5, x6, x9, xC union fields
diff: prototypes for mn_8022FB88, mn_8022FD18, mn_8022FEC8, mn_80230198, mn_80230274, fn_802309F0, mn_80230D18 updated

key_files.role: Incidental formatting cleanup in Home Run stage code; no matching impact shown for this file in the bot report.
evidence_refs: changed_files: src/melee/gr/grhomerun.c added 10 deleted 11
diff: line wrapping changes around grHomeRun_8021CB20, grHomeRun_8021D680, grHomeRun_8021E500

what_changed.text: Added recovered C implementations for mnmainrule functions including fn_8022F538, mn_8022FB88, mn_8022FD18, mn_8022FEC8, mn_80230198, and mn_80230274, with further diff evidence showing code added through mn_80230E38-related creation logic.
evidence_refs: diff: placeholder comments replaced by C function bodies in src/melee/mn/mnmainrule.c

what_changed.text: Recovered menu-rule data layout in the header by replacing padding-only fields with named byte fields and a union exposing HSD_JObj pointer arrays/embedded object slots.
evidence_refs: diff: mnmainrule.h struct mn_802307F8_t x3_pad replaced by x3, x4, x5, x6, x7_pad, x9
diff: mnmainrule.h adds union with HSD_JObj* xC[17], xC0[10], x34[7]

what_changed.text: Replaced unknown prototypes with typed function signatures for several mnmainrule functions.
evidence_refs: diff: UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS removed for mn_8022FB88, mn_8022FD18, mn_8022FEC8, mn_80230198, mn_80230274, fn_802309F0
diff: mn_80230D18 now takes struct mn_802307F8_t*, HSD_JObj*, s8

what_changed.text: Added mnmainrule includes needed by the recovered code, including gobj object/userdata, gm_18A5, gm_1A36, jobj, memory, and debug headers.
evidence_refs: diff: new includes at top of src/melee/mn/mnmainrule.c

what_changed.text: Automated report recorded 2 new matches: mn_80230198 at 100.00% (+220 bytes) and mn_802307F8 at 100.00% (+35 bytes).
evidence_refs: text_corpus issue_comment decomp-dev[bot] 2026-04-30T17:42:32Z

what_changed.text: Automated report recorded a .sdata2 regression in mnmainrule: 50.00% to 36.36%, -10 bytes.
evidence_refs: text_corpus issue_comment decomp-dev[bot] regression table

smart_moves.text: Updated the owning header as function bodies became known instead of keeping source-local unknown declarations.
evidence_refs: diff: mnmainrule.h UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS replaced with typed prototypes
decomp_standards: truthful-headers-and-includes

smart_moves.text: Used local temporary structs and typed fields to express recovered offsets in menu user data and JObj references, reducing reliance on raw offsets in some call paths.
evidence_refs: diff: struct mn_8022FB88_arg1_t with x58, xA8, xAC
diff: struct mn_8022FEC8_jobj_ref_t with x10
diff: mnmainrule.h expands struct mn_802307F8_t

smart_moves.text: Represented repeated JObj flag operations and menu option scans with loops rather than repeated generated-looking blocks.
evidence_refs: diff: mn_8022FB88 do-while loops over four entries
diff: mn_8022FD18 for loops over packed index bytes
diff: mn_80230274 loops over option count and visible entries
decomp_standards: natural-loops

decomp_lessons.lesson: For menu user-data structs, recovering even partial byte fields and JObj pointer arrays can unlock typed prototypes and large matching gains while leaving unknown padding explicit.
evidence_refs: diff: struct mn_802307F8_t expanded with byte fields and HSD_JObj union
diff: function prototypes updated from unknown to typed
bot_report: multiple mnmainrule functions improved or matched

decomp_lessons.lesson: Packed small integer data can be modeled as stack scalars cast to u8* when the recovered code iterates over byte indices used to select JObjs.
evidence_refs: diff: sp14 = mn_804DBDFC; var_r29 = (u8*) &sp14; loop indexes data->x58[*var_r29]
diff: sp14 and spC loaded from mn_804DBE04/mn_804DBE08 then iterated through u8* ptr

decomp_lessons.lesson: A narrow PAD_STACK can be part of a matched function, but the evidence here only supports its use for mn_80230198, where the automated report shows a 100% match.
evidence_refs: diff: mn_80230198 contains PAD_STACK(8)
decomp_standards: matching-tactics-need-evidence

naming_conventions.text: Unproven globals and functions kept address-style names such as mn_804D6BD0, mn_803EC600, mn_8022FEC8, and fn_8022F538.
evidence_refs: diff: numerous externs and functions retain mn_/fn_ address-style names
decomp_standards: conservative-naming

naming_conventions.text: Partially recovered structs use address/function-derived names plus offset-style fields, e.g. struct mn_8022FB88_arg1_t with x58, xA8, xAC and struct mn_8022FEC8_jobj_ref_t with x10.
evidence_refs: diff: local struct declarations near top of mnmainrule.c

naming_conventions.text: Clear local roles appear with short names such as data, rules, buttons, hovered, limits, jobj, roots, indices, option_roots, selected, and visible.
evidence_refs: diff: fn_8022F538 and mn_80230274 local declarations

assembly_or_matching_tactics.text: Used typed temporary structs and header layout expansion to describe offsets into menu data while preserving unknown padding.
evidence_refs: diff: struct mn_8022FB88_arg1_t pad ranges and x58/xA8/xAC
diff: mnmainrule.h struct mn_802307F8_t union over xC

assembly_or_matching_tactics.text: Used byte-pointer views of packed stack/global values to drive JObj index loops.
evidence_refs: diff: var_r29 = (u8*) &sp14 in mn_8022FB88
diff: ptr = (u8*) &sp14 and ptr = (u8*) &spC in mn_8022FD18

assembly_or_matching_tactics.text: Used a goto-shaped range filter in mn_80230198 and a PAD_STACK(8), with automated evidence that the function became a 100% match.
evidence_refs: diff: mn_80230198 contains labels seven and func plus PAD_STACK(8)

assembly_or_matching_tactics.text: Added explicit animation frame tables mn_803EC734 and mn_803EC770 used by mn_80230198 to choose AnimLoopSettings.
evidence_refs: diff: AnimLoopSettings mn_803EC734[5] and mn_803EC770[9]
diff: mn_80230198 scans both tables and calls mn_8022ED6C

notes: PR body is empty, so author intent beyond the title and diff is unavailable.
No human review comments were present; review feedback is limited to the automated bot report.
The loaded diff excerpt and raw_diff are truncated in the prompt, but targeted lookup recovered the mnmainrule.h hunk and enough surrounding diff to classify key changes.
No local build, objdiff, or full regression logs beyond the bot report were loaded; match claims are limited to the bot-reported tables.
The grhomerun.c changes appear formatting-only from the shown diff, but no explicit comment confirms intent.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Partial menu user-data typing can drive matching and header cleanup
accepted_candidate_records.text: In PR 2444, expanding mnmainrule user-data structs and replacing unknown prototypes coincided with 2 new mnmainrule matches and multiple improvement scores. Keep this as a PR-specific lesson: partial typed fields and explicit padding can be useful when grounded by recovered bodies.
evidence_refs: diff: mnmainrule.h struct/prototype expansion
diff: mnmainrule.c recovered function bodies
bot_report: 2 new matches and 10 improvements

accepted_candidate_records.title: PAD_STACK(8) in mn_80230198
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR 2444 used PAD_STACK(8) in mn_80230198, and the bot report marked mn_80230198 as a new 100% match. Treat as narrow evidence for this function only, not as a global style preference.
evidence_refs: diff: PAD_STACK(8) in mn_80230198

rejection_notes: Do not infer that the .sdata2 regression was acceptable or intentional; the loaded evidence only shows that it was reported and the PR later merged.
Do not generalize the goto-shaped control flow in mn_80230198 as preferred style; it is only tied to this matched function in the available evidence.
Do not promote address-style mn_/fn_ names as semantic names; the PR preserves them where meaning is not proven.
Do not treat grhomerun.c changes as decomp progress; the shown diff is formatting-only and the bot report lists no grhomerun matching result.
